shortTitle: Pov: You're The Worst Mafia Boss Ever

content.text: This is what it feels like. You’re the boss now, the one they say runs the whole city—the kingpin, the nightmare under every bed.
content.imageDescription: A close-up of the protagonist standing at the top of a tall building overlooking a sprawling city at dawn, exuding confidence with the city lights fading and the sun beginning to rise, while people in the shadows below look up in apprehension and awe.

content.text: But sunrise comes, and your first order of business? You lose the ledgers. Not to the police, not to a rival—just misplace them, left inside the trunk of a car you can’t remember where you parked. Your crew stares at you like you’ve got two heads. Ice forms in your gut, cold, heavy, humiliating.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist frantically searches for a set of ledgers, patting their pockets and rummaging through papers, while their crew stands around, exchanging confused or judgmental glances. The scene is set in a dimly lit garage with various cars, and the protagonist appears anxious and embarrassed.

content.text: Next, a new recruit, eager to impress, asks for your signature on a hit. You give permission, not realizing he’s written it on a coffee receipt and now it’s evidence.
content.imageDescription: A wide shot at a cluttered office table: the protagonist signs a makeshift contract without looking closely, while the nervous, enthusiastic new recruit watches. On the table, a crumpled coffee receipt with a signature is clearly visible. The recruit quickly pockets the incriminating receipt, a sly grin on his face.

content.text: You want to scream, throw something, sack the whole crew. But you can’t. Your underbosses know. They smell the doubt, the hesitation—blood in the water. Your consigliere starts making calls behind your back.
content.imageDescription: A tense office: the protagonist clenches their fists in silent rage, staring at their crew mutinously. Across the room, a trusted consigliere can be seen whispering into a phone behind a partially closed door, while several underbosses exchange knowing looks and smirks.

content.text: Paranoia steeps your mind, turns your sleep into sweat-drenched nightmares. Your own cousin is paying protection to another crew.
content.imageDescription: A split scene: on one side, the protagonist tosses and turns in a messy bed, drenched in sweat, eyes wide open in fear. On the other, the protagonist’s cousin hands cash to a rival mobster at a secretive alleyway meetup, looking nervous and glancing over their shoulder.

content.text: You try to rally: shout, pound your fist, demand fear. Nobody listens. Not really. The street hustlers just laugh. You realize the truth stings sharper than a bullet. Power isn’t handed—it has to be earned, and tonight, you’re nothing but a joke in an empty suit.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist stands on a city street at night, shouting and slamming a fist on the hood of a car, trying to command authority. Small-time hustlers nearby snicker and point, ignoring the outburst. The protagonist’s reflection in a store window shows them disheveled and alone, suit rumpled—a powerful figure now looking lost and defeated.
